scenario: Multiple hard lockups with MCE events
symptoms: NMI watchdog: Watchdog detected hard LOCKUP on cpu 32
NMI watchdog: Watchdog detected hard LOCKUP on cpu 62
Kernel panic - not syncing: Hard LOCKUP

workflow.thought: Check kernel log for lockup messages
workflow.observation: Hard lockup messages on CPUs 32 and 62

workflow.action: list -H ffff88bddf31fd38 -o page.lru -s page.index | paste - - | wc -l

workflow.observation: List head next and prev pointers
CPU 8 runqueue with current and queued tasks
Soft lockup on CPU 23 before hard lockups

workflow.thought: Examine full kernel log for MCE events
workflow.observation: Multiple MCE events logged over 97 days
Significant timestamp delays on many CPUs

root_cause_analysis: Multiple hard lockups occurred on multiple CPUs on which tasks were running different code paths that had nothing to do with each other.

Lots of MCE events were being logged and also hrtimer/perf interrupts were taking too long time to be processed during the time frame of the occurrence of the soft lockup followed by the multiple hard lockups.

It may be possible that some faulty hardware was causing some CPUs to be getting slow or stalled on which tasks were processing some jobs with zone->lock or rq->lock spinlocks being held hence the soft/hard lockups.
